I know I sound like a broken record but worth repeating one more time:

If $SPX closes green tomorrow, it will be the longest daily winning streak of 10 days or greater since 1995.

If $SPX closes green this week, it will be the longest weekly winning streak of 10 weeks or greater since 1985.

80% of ya’ll reading this probably weren’t even alive in 1985 lol.

tweets.text:
Anyone who gave Sui a hard time this last week and has used Solana at all in the last several years has no ground stand on.

SOL has had seven major outages. The majority of them occurred within the first several years of its existence - in the midst of insane growth... and it continued to have rapid growth after.

The recent SUI outage occurred right after the latest version release which added gasless (aka free for the users) stablecoin features.

This is a massive upgrade for the network for revenue and for attracting capital. It's already seen a new high in yearly TPS and greater parity (with a few days flipping) with Solana in p2p stablecoin transfer volume.
tweets.full_text:
Anyone who gave Sui a hard time this last week and has used Solana at all in the last several years has no ground stand on.

SOL has had seven major outages. The majority of them occurred within the first several years of its existence - in the midst of insane growth... and it continued to have rapid growth after.

The recent SUI outage occurred right after the latest version release which added gasless (aka free for the users) stablecoin features.

This is a massive upgrade for the network for revenue and for attracting capital. It's already seen a new high in yearly TPS and greater parity (with a few days flipping) with Solana in p2p stablecoin transfer volume.

tweets.text: I wonder if a Bitcoin crash will be a harbinger of things to come in risk assets in general, or if it's just a one-off thing confined to Bitcoin and crypto. Whichever it is, we should find out soon enough. Maybe it will also be a catalyst to drive investors into value and safety.

tweets.text: There is way too much complacency in Bitcoin for the market to be anywhere near a bottom. When Bitcoin breaks $50K, it should be a quick fall below $20K, which should be a big enough drop to shake the conviction of long-term HODLers, causing many to finally throw in the towel.

tweets.text:
If you want to see an early peak at what happens when ubi hits, prices for the nba playoffs is a great example.  knicks game prices are so much more expensive than spurs games. 

why?  ny is a much wealthier city than san antonio.

now, under ubi, if much more people can afford to splurge on a game, but they cant make the stadium any bigger, prices skyrocket.  and only the richest of the rich will be able to attend.

owning scarce physical (and digital) goods that have tightly controlled supply either by design, or is just physically impossible (think real estate in desirable locations) will go absolutely parabolic.

prob still a little too early on this trade, but i think it will hold true within the next few years.
